rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Mizik (2010) to define and motivate the idea of marketing myopia under stock-market pressure. In the main text and Table 1, it relies on Mizik's finding that cuts in marketing and R&D can boost short-term valuations but harm longer-run firm value, which supports the paper's argument about myopic incentives faced by public firms. Although the citing study does not reuse Mizik's empirical design, Mizik (2010) materially informs the conceptual setup and hypothesis motivation for post-IPO innovation behavior.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Mizik (2010) as prior empirical grounding for the myopic-management mechanism, using it to justify why public-market pressure can shift firms away from riskier innovation choices. This role is part of the theoretical foundation for the citing paper's IPO and innovation hypotheses.
